conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You mentioned you used to enjoy walking the dog—what else has started to feel like a chore?
Cooking. I look at the recipe and just feel like I can't do it. I end up ordering takeout because it's just easier.
When you feel like you can't do something simple like cooking, what goes through your mind?
I just think I’m getting too old for this. I used to be so independent, and now I’m starting to wonder if I can keep up.
Can you tell me more about how that has been affecting your day-to-day?
It’s just a sense of losing control. I used to be able to handle things, but now I feel like I’m just trying to keep my head above water.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It feels like I’m watching myself from outside, just observing how tired I am. I look at my hands and I think, "That’s not my life anymore."
You mentioned looking at your hands and thinking "That's not my life anymore"—what feels different about your life now?
It’s just... I used to be the one in charge, and now I feel like I’m just trying to keep up. It’s hard to accept that I’m not the same person I was a few years ago.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
I think it was after the divorce. We were married for 20 years, and suddenly I was alone. It took me a while to realize I wasn't just missing my partner, but the life we had built together.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
The evenings. I used to have people over, or go out, but now I just sit here alone and watch the TV. It feels like I'm just waiting for something to happen, but I don't know what.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. I just want to get through the days without crying. I feel like I'm just existing until something changes, but I don't know what that change is.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
I think it's both. I'm exhausted from fighting this feeling, but at the same time, I'm just... done. I don't have the energy to pretend everything is fine when it's not.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about the past, does it make it harder to look forward to things during the day?
Yeah, it does. I just keep thinking about what could have gone wrong, and it makes me feel like I’m stuck in this loop where I can’t move forward.
You mentioned feeling stuck in a loop—has that made it harder to get excited about things you usually enjoy?
Yeah, completely. I used to love going out with friends, but now it just feels like a chore. I go through the motions, but I’m not really there.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It feels like I’m just pretending. I sit there and I'm just thinking about how I should be happy, but I’m not.
When you think about how you should be happy but you're not, what kind of thoughts come up about yourself?
That I’m just not good enough. That I must be doing something wrong, or I wouldn’t be sitting there feeling so miserable.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
After the breakup. It was like I lost a part of myself, and I don’t know how to get it back.
Can you tell me more about how that has been affecting your day-to-day?
It’s just... everything feels dull. Even the things I used to love, like my job or hobbies, just don't seem as interesting anymore.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
Just everything. Even the small things like cooking dinner or watching a movie feel like a chore.
When you think about not being good enough, how does that affect your view of the future?
It makes me scared to try again. I don't want to put myself in a situation where I end up feeling like that again.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't really know. I just know I need to get out of here and figure out what I want to do with my life.
